visa_types.overview:
Overview
Visas are issued upon invitations of Ukraine's  authorities or foreign states' authorities/international organizations.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Visas are issued upon invitations of Ukraine's  authorities or foreign states' authorities/international organizations.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Official visitor]
Visa fees for certain visa types for applicants from certain countries  might differ from basic visa fees above due to reciprocity principle.
Double rates of consular fee shall apply in case of urgent processing.
Kindly note that service fees is 29 Dollar per applicant.
Fees  are  (visa-fees-at-glance.html)not refunded  in case of a visa refusal
Issuance of B, C, and D visas by  a Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall be subject to consular fee at the  rate of 65 US dollars, unless otherwise stipulated by  international treaties of Ukraine.
When a foreign state charges a  consular fee for issuance of visas to Ukrainian citizens in the amount  exceeding the mentioned rate, the Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall  charge a consular fee for visa issuance to the nationals of this state on the  reciprocity principle. In this regard we encourage applicants to specify the  applicable visa consular fee directly at the embassies of Ukraine, where they  intend to obtain visas.
Urgent visa issuance or visa  issuance during non-working hours shall be subject to a double consular fee  rate.
Paid consular fees are  not subject to refund.
Visas shall be issued with zero  rate of consular fee to:
- children  under 6 years of age;
- foreign  Ukrainians, subject to showing foreign Ukrainian’s ID, foreign Ukrainian’s  spouse and children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine on business and are holders of a diplomatic or service  passport;
- staff  of Ukraine’s diplomatic missions abroad and consular posts, and their family  members;
- staff  of international organizations and their representations in Ukraine, and their  family members;
- staff  of states’ liaison offices at international organizations that have  headquarters in Ukraine who, according to the statutes of such organizations or  relevant international treaties, enjoy diplomatic privileges and immunity, and  their family members;
- heads  and members of official delegations of foreign states and accompanying persons  who enter Ukraine upon invitation of the President of Ukraine, Verkhovna Rada  of Ukraine, Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine, Presidential Administration,  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.
- honorary  consuls of Ukraine, his/her spouse and minor children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine with the purpose of participating in the implementation of  international technical assistance projects registered according to the  established procedure, upon invitation of a government institution, enterprise  or organization identified as recipients of the project;
- staff  of the emergency and rescue forces of foreign states who enter Ukraine with the  purpose of responding to an emergency, upon request of the State Emergency  Service;
- representatives  of foreign military formations and institutions who enter Ukraine on service  duty, upon invitation of a relevant government body;
- official  observers from foreign states and international organizations registered by the  Central Election Commission to observe presidential elections, parliamentary  elections, local elections and national referendums;
- other  persons who enter Ukraine with the aim to ensure its foreign and internal  policy interests or with a humanitarian purpose, upon written decision of a  head of a Ukrainian diplomatic mission aboard or consular department of the  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.

## Documents Required
- signed visa application form;
- applicant’s valid passport/travel document;
- 1 applicant’s photo (35 * 45 mm);
- residence documents – valid residence permits/equivalents (in cases where applicants reside in a country that is different from the country of their nationality/citizenship);
- a letter of invitation issued by Ukraine’s ministries / other central executive authorities, state entities and organizations or relevant letters of foreign states’ authorities / international organizations
- Please note that applicants might be asked for additional documents by Ukraine’s visa officers (e.g. return flight tickets and other documents confirming applicants’ circumstances (to be defined by Ukraine’s visa officers of the relevant Embassy/Consulate General).

## Processing Time
''Visa is issued within 9-10 WORKING DAYS
If an applicant possesses:
- a passport with a valid used multi-entry visa of Schengen countries, Australia, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Japan, or possesses residency permit in one of the above countries;
- a passport with a visa for entry to Ukraine that was used within the last 24 months
visa could be issued within 6-7 working days.
This period may be extended up to 30 working days if additional checks are necessary"

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the  application form should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up to date photograph (not more than 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- The size of the face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline       must be between 31mm and 36mm
- The background shall be plain and light coloured
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for       religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photos that do not  comply with these standards are not accepted.

## Visa Application Form
Submit you visa application online (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Download visa  application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Qt59gtqnpcPEAY5npiq74/76f4a10ccfbd35c3a62d62c677ec2ca1/visa-application-form.pdf)
Requested documents (http://lebanon.mfa.gov.ua/en/consular-affairs/services/visas/Short+list)

visa_types.name: Private visit
visa_types.documents_required:
1. Passport + copies (residence permit if not Lebanese + copy)
Passport should meet the following requirements:
Be valid for at least three months after the stated date of departure from Ukraine;
Have at least two blank visa designated pages;
For minors - + copies of parents’ passport
Copies of all previous (valid and expired) visas and used pages must be made.
2. Completed visa application form from the website http://visa.mfa.gov.ua (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Visa application forms of minors shall be submitted for each person
3. Photo
Photo must be in color, 35x45 mm, printed on normal photographic paper, taken against a light background; full face taken straight forward; the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed. The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead.
4. Valid health insurance with coverage of at least 30,000 EUR or equivalent in other currency. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
5. Bank account issued not later than 3 months (at the rate of 1000 USD per 15 days stay in Ukraine)
bank account must be originally stamped. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
6. Employment letter/Student/commercial circular/ ID for engineers/pharmacists/attorneys etc.
7. Family register (issued not later than 6 months before the applying date)
8. Notary invitation from the inviting person.
It must be issued by Ukrainian notary.
MANDATORY requirements for the invitation:
Information of the inviting person - name, surname, passport data (series, number, date of issue, issuing authority) and a permanent or temporary residence (if not Ukrainian), address of residence in Ukraine, mobile phone, the commitment of an inviting person to cover all expenses of invited person during his/her stay in Ukraine and his/her departure from Ukraine
Information of the invited person - name, surname, information about its date and place of birth, nationality, passport data (series, number, date of issue, issuing authority), address in Lebanon, purpose of the trip (private), the period of the planned visit to Ukraine, the number of entries and the place of his/her residence in Ukraine.
9. Copies of internal Ukrainian passport / residence permit (if not Ukrainian) of the inviting person – all pages with marks and stamps needed!
10. Booked round trip air tickets from Lebanon to one of the countries bordering Ukraine, namely Poland / Slovakia / Hungary / Romania / Moldova + booked round trip train / bus tickets to Ukraine from one of the selected "transit" countries
Documents in Arabic must be translated into Ukrainian or English
Please note:
In case of the visa rejection consular fees are non-refundable.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents.
The applicant may be requested to appear in the Embassy for interview.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Family reunification with foreigner or stateless person legally residing in Ukraine.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Family reunion with foreigners
                                  ]
Visa fees for certain visa types for applicants from certain countries  might differ from basic visa fees above due to reciprocity principle.
Double rates of consular fee shall apply in case of urgent processing.
Kindly note that service fees is 29 Dollar per applicant.
Fees  are  (visa-fees-at-glance.html)not refunded  in case of a visa refusal
Issuance of B, C, and D visas by  a Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall be subject to consular fee at the  rate of 65 US dollars, unless otherwise stipulated by  international treaties of Ukraine.
When a foreign state charges a  consular fee for issuance of visas to Ukrainian citizens in the amount  exceeding the mentioned rate, the Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall  charge a consular fee for visa issuance to the nationals of this state on the  reciprocity principle. In this regard we encourage applicants to specify the  applicable visa consular fee directly at the embassies of Ukraine, where they  intend to obtain visas.
Urgent visa issuance or visa  issuance during non-working hours shall be subject to a double consular fee  rate.
Paid consular fees are  not subject to refund.
Visas shall be issued with zero  rate of consular fee to:
- children  under 6 years of age;
- foreign  Ukrainians, subject to showing foreign Ukrainian’s ID, foreign Ukrainian’s  spouse and children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine on business and are holders of a diplomatic or service  passport;
- staff  of Ukraine’s diplomatic missions abroad and consular posts, and their family  members;
- staff  of international organizations and their representations in Ukraine, and their  family members;
- staff  of states’ liaison offices at international organizations that have  headquarters in Ukraine who, according to the statutes of such organizations or  relevant international treaties, enjoy diplomatic privileges and immunity, and  their family members;
- heads  and members of official delegations of foreign states and accompanying persons  who enter Ukraine upon invitation of the President of Ukraine, Verkhovna Rada  of Ukraine, Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine, Presidential Administration,  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.
- honorary  consuls of Ukraine, his/her spouse and minor children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine with the purpose of participating in the implementation of  international technical assistance projects registered according to the  established procedure, upon invitation of a government institution, enterprise  or organization identified as recipients of the project;
- staff  of the emergency and rescue forces of foreign states who enter Ukraine with the  purpose of responding to an emergency, upon request of the State Emergency  Service;
- representatives  of foreign military formations and institutions who enter Ukraine on service  duty, upon invitation of a relevant government body;
- official  observers from foreign states and international organizations registered by the  Central Election Commission to observe presidential elections, parliamentary  elections, local elections and national referendums;
- other  persons who enter Ukraine with the aim to ensure its foreign and internal  policy interests or with a humanitarian purpose, upon written decision of a  head of a Ukrainian diplomatic mission aboard or consular department of the  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.

## Documents Required
- signed visa application form;
- applicant’s valid passport/travel document;
- 1 applicant’s photo (35 * 45 mm);
- residence documents – valid residence permits/equivalents (in cases where applicants reside in a country that is different from the country of their nationality/citizenship);
- proof that the applicant is a holder of (or is entitled to benefit from) a valid medical/travel insurance policy to cover Ukraine and the period of stay (the policy should
                                                                                    provide a minimum of €30 000 or equivalent coverage);
- proof of sufficient funds to cover the costs of stay (e.g. letter from the applicant’s employer/spouse confirming their coverage of applicant’s expenses in Ukraine, bank statement
                                                                                    for 2 recent months, bank cards along with current/savings account balance statements provided the bank cards can be cashed in Ukraine, cash in Ukrainian hryvnia/other
                                                                                    convertible currency, accommodation arrangements etc).
- one of the following:
document(s) confirming that the applicant is a family member of a foreigner/stateless person residing in Ukraine upon a temporary residence permit accompanied by a copy of that foreigner/stateless person’s temporary residence permit as well as proof of that foreigner/stateless person’s sufficient funds to cover applicant’s expenses in Ukraine (e.g. bank statement for 2 recent months, bank cards along with current/savings account balance statements provided the bank cards can be cashed in Ukraine, cash in Ukrainian hryvnia/other convertible currency, accommodation arrangements etc.). Certificates issued by foreign countries should be apostilled (legalized) and translated into Ukrainian as well as duly certified;
or 
document(s) confirming that the applicant is a family member of a foreigner/stateless person who has been granted refuge/temporary protection in Ukraine. Certificates issued by foreign countries should be apostilled (legalized) and translated into Ukrainian as well as duly certified.
Please note that applicants might be asked for additional documents by Ukraine’s visa officers (e.g. return flight tickets and other documents confirming applicants’ circumstances (to be defined by Ukraine’s visa officers of the relevant Embassy/Consulate General).

## Processing Time
''Visa is issued within 9-10 WORKING DAYS
If an applicant possesses:
- a passport with a valid used multi-entry visa of Schengen countries, Australia, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Japan, or possesses residency permit in one of the above countries;
- a passport with a visa for entry to Ukraine that was used within the last 24 months
visa could be issued within 6-7 working days.
This period may be extended up to 30 working days if additional checks are necessary"

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the  application form should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up to date photograph (not more than 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- The size of the face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline       must be between 31mm and 36mm
- The background shall be plain and light coloured
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for       religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photos that do not  comply with these standards are not accepted.

visa_types.name: Business visit
visa_types.documents_required:
1. Passport + copies (residence permit if not Lebanese + copy)
Passport should meet the following requirements:
Be valid for at least three months after the stated date of departure from Ukraine;
Have at least two blank visa designated pages;
For minors - + copies of parents’ passport
Copies of all previous (valid and expired) visas and used pages must be made.
2. Completed visa application form from the website http://visa.mfa.gov.ua (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Visa application forms of minors shall be submitted for each person
3. Photo
Photo must be in color, 35x45 mm, printed on normal photographic paper, taken against a light background; full face taken straight forward; the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed. The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead.
4. Valid health insurance with coverage of at least 30,000 EUR or equivalent in other currency. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
5. Bank account issued not later than 3 months (at the rate of 1000 USD per 15 days stay in Ukraine)
bank account must be originally stamped. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
6. Employment letter/Student/commercial circular/ ID for engineers/pharmacists/attorneys etc.
7. Family register (issued not later than 6 months before the applying date)
8. Invitation issued by entity/legal body
MANDATORY requirements for the invitation:
shall be issued on the official letterhead/blank/form of a legal entity, must contain the number in the Unified State Register of Legal Entities (ЄДРПОУ), registration number, date and reference number, signed by the CEO / owner (their deputies) of the legal entity, a mobile phone number of a contact person, the commitment of an inviting entity to cover all possible expenses of the invited person during his/her stay in Ukraine and his/her departure from Ukraine.
Information of the invited person - name, surname, information about its date and place of birth, nationality, passport data (series, number, date of issue, issuing authority), address in Lebanon, the period of the planned visit to Ukraine, the number of entries and the place where he/she will live during his trip to Ukraine.
9. Extract from the Unified State Register of Legal Entities, Individual Entrepreneurs and Public Associations of the employer of the inviting party
10. Booked round trip air tickets from Lebanon to one of the countries bordering Ukraine, namely Poland / Slovakia / Hungary / Romania / Moldova + booked round trip train / bus tickets to Ukraine from one of the selected "transit" countries
Documents in Arabic must be translated into Ukrainian or English
Please note:
In case of the visa rejection consular fees are non-refundable.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents.
The applicant may be requested to appear in the Embassy for interview.

Visa fees for certain visa types for applicants from certain countries  might differ from basic visa fees above due to reciprocity principle.
Double rates of consular fee shall apply in case of urgent processing.
Kindly note that service fees is 29 Dollar per applicant.
Fees  are  (visa-fees-at-glance.html)not refunded  in case of a visa refusal
Issuance of B, C, and D visas by  a Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall be subject to consular fee at the  rate of 65 US dollars, unless otherwise stipulated by  international treaties of Ukraine.
When a foreign state charges a  consular fee for issuance of visas to Ukrainian citizens in the amount  exceeding the mentioned rate, the Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall  charge a consular fee for visa issuance to the nationals of this state on the  reciprocity principle. In this regard we encourage applicants to specify the  applicable visa consular fee directly at the embassies of Ukraine, where they  intend to obtain visas.
Urgent visa issuance or visa  issuance during non-working hours shall be subject to a double consular fee  rate.
Paid consular fees are  not subject to refund.
Visas shall be issued with zero  rate of consular fee to:
- children  under 6 years of age;
- foreign  Ukrainians, subject to showing foreign Ukrainian’s ID, foreign Ukrainian’s  spouse and children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine on business and are holders of a diplomatic or service  passport;
- staff  of Ukraine’s diplomatic missions abroad and consular posts, and their family  members;
- staff  of international organizations and their representations in Ukraine, and their  family members;
- staff  of states’ liaison offices at international organizations that have  headquarters in Ukraine who, according to the statutes of such organizations or  relevant international treaties, enjoy diplomatic privileges and immunity, and  their family members;
- heads  and members of official delegations of foreign states and accompanying persons  who enter Ukraine upon invitation of the President of Ukraine, Verkhovna Rada  of Ukraine, Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine, Presidential Administration,  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.
- honorary  consuls of Ukraine, his/her spouse and minor children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine with the purpose of participating in the implementation of  international technical assistance projects registered according to the  established procedure, upon invitation of a government institution, enterprise  or organization identified as recipients of the project;
- staff  of the emergency and rescue forces of foreign states who enter Ukraine with the  purpose of responding to an emergency, upon request of the State Emergency  Service;
- representatives  of foreign military formations and institutions who enter Ukraine on service  duty, upon invitation of a relevant government body;
- official  observers from foreign states and international organizations registered by the  Central Election Commission to observe presidential elections, parliamentary  elections, local elections and national referendums;
- other  persons who enter Ukraine with the aim to ensure its foreign and internal  policy interests or with a humanitarian purpose, upon written decision of a  head of a Ukrainian diplomatic mission aboard or consular department of the  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.

## Documents Required
- Completed visa application form from the website
 
http://visa.mfa.gov.ua (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
- applicant’s valid passport/travel document; (should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of departure from Ukraine and have at least 2 pages free from any visas/marks/stamps) and copy of its first page
- 1 applicant’s color photo (size – 35х45 mm. with white background).
- residence documents – valid residence permits for minimum 3 months after departure date from Ukraine (in cases where applicants reside in a country that is different from the country of their nationality/citizenship);
- proof that the applicant is a holder of (or is entitled to benefit from) a valid medical/travel insurance policy to cover Ukraine and the period of stay (the policy should provide a minimum of €30 000 or equivalent coverage);
- proof of sufficient funds to cover the costs of stay (e.g. bank statement for 6 recent months, cash in Ukrainian hryvnia/other convertible currency, bank cards along with current/savings account balance statements provided the bank cards can be cashed in Ukraine, hotel/accommodation booking, letter from the applicant’s employer/sponsor confirming their coverage of applicant’s expenses in Ukraine, a return/transit ticket etc);
- Invitation letter issued by Ukraine’s hospital/medical treatment institution.
Please note that applicants might be asked for additional documents by Ukraine’s visa officers (e.g. return flight tickets and other documents confirming applicants’ circumstances (to be defined by Ukraine’s visa officers of the relevant Embassy/Consulate General).

## Processing Time
''Visa is issued within 9-10 WORKING DAYS
If an applicant possesses:
- a passport with a valid used multi-entry visa of Schengen countries, Australia, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Japan, or possesses residency permit in one of the above countries;
- a passport with a visa for entry to Ukraine that was used within the last 24 months
visa could be issued within 6-7 working days.
This period may be extended up to 30 working days if additional checks are necessary"

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the  application form should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up to date photograph (not more than 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- The size of the face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline       must be between 31mm and 36mm
- The background shall be plain and light coloured
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for       religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photos that do not  comply with these standards are not accepted.

Overview
Transit visa (type B) - can be issued as singe-entry, two-entry and multi-entry visas for a period indicated in documents serving as a basis for visa issuance, but for no longer than one year. visa_types.name: Tourism
visa_types.documents_required:
1. Passport + copies (residence permit if not Lebanese + copy)
Passport should meet the following requirements:
Be valid for at least three months after the stated date of departure from Ukraine;
Have at least two blank visa designated pages;
For minors - + copies of parents’ passport
Copies of all previous (valid and expired) visas and used pages must be made.
2. Completed visa application form from the website http://visa.mfa.gov.ua (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Visa application forms of minors shall be submitted for each person

The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead.
4. Valid health insurance with coverage of at least 30,000 EUR or equivalent in other currency. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
5. Bank account issued not later than 3 months (at the rate of 1000 USD per 15 days stay in Ukraine)
bank account must be originally stamped. Holders of diplomatic or service passport shall be exempt from this requirement;
6. Employment letter/Student/commercial circular/ ID for engineers/pharmacists/attorneys etc.
7. Family register (issued not later than 6 months before the applying date)
8. Documents confirming that the nature of travel is tourism in accordance with the Law of Ukraine “On Tourism”
- agreement on tourist services with Ukrainian tourist operator, tourist voucher is mandatory.
- hotel reservation
9. Booked round trip air tickets from Lebanon to one of the countries bordering Ukraine, namely Poland / Slovakia / Hungary / Romania / Moldova + booked round trip train / bus tickets to Ukraine from one of the selected "transit" countries
Documents in Arabic must be translated into Ukrainian or English
Please note:
In case of the visa rejection consular fees are non-refundable.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents.
The applicant may be requested to appear in the Embassy for interview.

Visa for owners of real estate in Ukraine.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Owner of property in
                                    Ukraine]
Visa fees for certain visa types for applicants from certain countries  might differ from basic visa fees above due to reciprocity principle.
Double rates of consular fee shall apply in case of urgent processing.
Kindly note that service fees is 29 Dollar per applicant.
Fees  are  (visa-fees-at-glance.html)not refunded  in case of a visa refusal
Issuance of B, C, and D visas by  a Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall be subject to consular fee at the  rate of 65 US dollars, unless otherwise stipulated by  international treaties of Ukraine.
When a foreign state charges a  consular fee for issuance of visas to Ukrainian citizens in the amount  exceeding the mentioned rate, the Ukrainian diplomatic mission abroad shall  charge a consular fee for visa issuance to the nationals of this state on the  reciprocity principle. In this regard we encourage applicants to specify the  applicable visa consular fee directly at the embassies of Ukraine, where they  intend to obtain visas.
Urgent visa issuance or visa  issuance during non-working hours shall be subject to a double consular fee  rate.
Paid consular fees are  not subject to refund.
Visas shall be issued with zero  rate of consular fee to:
- children  under 6 years of age;
- foreign  Ukrainians, subject to showing foreign Ukrainian’s ID, foreign Ukrainian’s  spouse and children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine on business and are holders of a diplomatic or service  passport;
- staff  of Ukraine’s diplomatic missions abroad and consular posts, and their family  members;
- staff  of international organizations and their representations in Ukraine, and their  family members;
- staff  of states’ liaison offices at international organizations that have  headquarters in Ukraine who, according to the statutes of such organizations or  relevant international treaties, enjoy diplomatic privileges and immunity, and  their family members;
- heads  and members of official delegations of foreign states and accompanying persons  who enter Ukraine upon invitation of the President of Ukraine, Verkhovna Rada  of Ukraine, Cabinet of Ministers of Ukraine, Presidential Administration,  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.
- honorary  consuls of Ukraine, his/her spouse and minor children;
- persons  who enter Ukraine with the purpose of participating in the implementation of  international technical assistance projects registered according to the  established procedure, upon invitation of a government institution, enterprise  or organization identified as recipients of the project;
- staff  of the emergency and rescue forces of foreign states who enter Ukraine with the  purpose of responding to an emergency, upon request of the State Emergency  Service;
- representatives  of foreign military formations and institutions who enter Ukraine on service  duty, upon invitation of a relevant government body;
- official  observers from foreign states and international organizations registered by the  Central Election Commission to observe presidential elections, parliamentary  elections, local elections and national referendums;
- other  persons who enter Ukraine with the aim to ensure its foreign and internal  policy interests or with a humanitarian purpose, upon written decision of a  head of a Ukrainian diplomatic mission aboard or consular department of the  Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine.

## Documents Required
- signed visa application form;
- applicant’s valid passport/travel document;
- 1 applicant’s photo (35 * 45 mm);
- residence documents – valid residence permits/equivalents (in cases where applicants reside in a country that is different from the country of their nationality/citizenship);
- proof that a foreigner or stateless person owns property (real estate) allocated in Ukraine;
- proof of medical/travel insurance to cover Ukraine and the period of stay (the policy should provide 30 000 euros or equivalent minimum coverage);
- proof of sufficient funds to cover the costs of stay (e.g. bank statement for 2 recent months, cash in Ukrainian hryvnia/other convertible currency, bank cards along with current/savings account balance statements provided the bank cards can be cashed in Ukraine, hotel/accommodation booking, letter from the applicant’s employer/sponsor confirming their coverage of applicant’s expenses in Ukraine, a return/transit ticket etc).
Please note that applicants might be asked for additional documents by Ukraine’s visa officers (e.g. return flight tickets and other documents confirming applicants’ circumstances (to be defined by Ukraine’s visa officers of the relevant Embassy/Consulate General).

## Processing Time
''Visa is issued within 9-10 WORKING DAYS
If an applicant possesses:
- a passport with a valid used multi-entry visa of Schengen countries, Australia, the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, Ireland, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Japan, or possesses residency permit in one of the above countries;
- a passport with a visa for entry to Ukraine that was used within the last 24 months
visa could be issued within 6-7 working days.
This period may be extended up to 30 working days if additional checks are necessary"

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the  application form should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up to date photograph (not more than 6 months old)
- Must be a colour photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 х 4,5 сm
- Face should take 70-80% of the photograph
- The size of the face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline       must be between 31mm and 36mm
- The background shall be plain and light coloured
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for       religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed
Please note that photos that do not  comply with these standards are not accepted.

## Visa Application Form
Submit you visa application online (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Download visa  application (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2Qt59gtqnpcPEAY5npiq74/76f4a10ccfbd35c3a62d62c677ec2ca1/visa-application-form.pdf)
Requested documents (http://lebanon.mfa.gov.ua/en/consular-affairs/services/visas/Short+list)

visa_types.name: Study Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
1. Passport + copies (residence permit if not Lebanese + copy)
Passport should meet the following requirements:
Be valid for at least three months after the stated date of departure from Ukraine;
Have at least two blank visa designated pages;
For minors - + copies of parents’ passport
Copies of all previous (valid and expired) visas and used pages must be made.
2. Completed visa application form from the website http://visa.mfa.gov.ua (http://visa.mfa.gov.ua/)
Visa application forms of minors shall be submitted for each person
3. Photo
Photo must be in color, 35x45 mm, printed on normal photographic paper, taken against a light background; full face taken straight forward; the person is to be captured with neutral facial expression, with his/her eyes open and mouth closed. The photograph of a person, who for religious or medical reasons is wearing a head cover, must show the person's cheeks, chin and forehead.
4. Valid health insurance with coverage of at least 30,000 EUR or equivalent in other currency.
5. Parents’/relatives’ bank statement for the last 3 months + notary statement of the guarantor to cover all the expenses
Bank statement must be originally stamped + copy of the guarantor’s passport
6. Family register (issued not later than 3 months before the applying date)
7. Invitation for study + copy
It is issued ONLY by Ukrainian State Center for International Education (www.studyinukraine.gov.ua, e-mail: 
info@studyinukraine.gov.ua, +380443590539).
Legalized individual statement + copy
Abovementioned documents must be legalized at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Lebanon and translated ONLY! into Ukrainian!
9. 2 additional passport copies (only the 1st page)
10. Booked round trip air tickets from Lebanon to one of the countries bordering Ukraine, namely Poland / Slovakia / Hungary / Romania / Moldova + booked round trip train / bus tickets to Ukraine from one of the selected "transit" countries
Documents in Arabic must be translated into Ukrainian or English
Please note:
In case of the visa rejection consular fees are non-refundable.
The Embassy reserves the right to ask for additional documents.
The applicant may be requested to appear in the Embassy for interview.
